topic: Area of Study 5: Conventions of Pop
subject: music

items.q: What is a tone row in serial music?
items.correct: An ordered series of all 12 chromatic pitches, used as the basis of a piece.
items.misconception: A row of notes played together as a chord.
items.random_wrong: Any sequence of notes a composer chooses.
items.why: Students miss the SPECIFIC structure. Schoenberg's 12-tone technique uses ALL 12 pitches in a fixed order before any repeats, generating melodies and harmonies from that row.

items.q: How many crotchets are in a semibreve?
items.why: Students confuse the relative durations. A semibreve (whole note) = 2 minims = 4 crotchets = 8 quavers. Each level halves in length.

items.correct: A recurring main theme (A) alternates with contrasting episodes (B, C, etc.).
items.misconception: A round where voices enter at different times.
items.why: Students confuse rondo (alternating refrain) with round (canon). Rondo: ABACA, ABACABA, etc. The A theme keeps coming back, like a refrain.

items.q: A dot after a note adds?
items.why: Correct answer: A half of its value. “A quarter of its value” sounds right because it borrows the language of the topic — but it fails this question. Re-read the notes on why.

items.q: How many quavers equal a crotchet?
items.why: Exact answer: 2 quavers. Estimation gets you near it; only the anchored number survives the exam hall.
